unit.comment: /*************************************************************************** * _ _ ____ _ * Project ___| | | | _ \| | * / __| | | | |_) | | * | (__| |_| | _ <| |___ * \___|\___/|_| \_\_____| * * Copyright (C) Daniel Stenberg, <daniel@haxx.se>, et al. * * This software is licensed as described in the file COPYING, which * you should have received as part of this distribution. The terms * are also available at https://curl.se/docs/copyright.html. * * You may opt to use, copy, modify, merge, publish, distribute and/or sell * copies of the Software, and permit persons to whom the Software is * furnished to do so, under the terms of the COPYING file. * * This software is distributed on an "AS IS" basis, WITHOUT WARRANTY OF ANY * KIND, either express or implied. * * SPDX-License-Identifier: curl * ***************************************************************************/
/* for failf function prototype */
/* for Curl_getconnectinfo */
/* The last 3 #include files should be in this order */
/* true globals -- for curl_global_init() and curl_global_cleanup() */
/* * strdup (and other memory functions) is redefined in complicated * ways, but at this point it must be defined as the system-supplied strdup * so the callback pointer is initialized correctly. */
/* MSVC extension, dllimport identity */
/* * If a memory-using function (like curl_getenv) is used before * curl_global_init() is called, we need to have these pointers set already. */
/* MSVC extension, dllimport identity */
/** * curl_global_init() globally initializes curl given a bitwise set of the * different features of what to initialize. */

function.name: global_init

unit.comment: /** * curl_global_init() globally initializes curl given a bitwise set of the * different features of what to initialize. */

function.name: curl_global_init

unit.comment: /* * curl_global_init_mem() globally initializes curl and also registers the * user provided callback routines. */

function.name: curl_global_init_mem
function.parameter_list: (long flags, curl_malloc_callback m, curl_free_callback f, curl_realloc_callback r, curl_strdup_callback s, curl_calloc_callback c)

unit.comment: /** * curl_global_cleanup() globally cleanups curl, uses the value of * "easy_init_flags" to determine what needs to be cleaned up and what doesn't. */

function.name: curl_global_cleanup
curl_global_trace
curl_global_sslset

unit.comment: /* * curl_easy_init() is the external interface to alloc, setup and init an * easy handle that is returned. If anything goes wrong, NULL is returned. */

function.name: curl_easy_init

unit.comment: /* events_timer * * Callback that gets called with a new value when the timeout should be * updated. */

function.name: events_timer
function.parameter_list: (struct Curl_multi *multi, /* multi handle */ long timeout_ms, /* see above */ void *userp)

unit.comment: /* poll2cselect * * convert from poll() bit definitions to libcurl's CURL_CSELECT_* ones */

function.name: poll2cselect

unit.comment: /* socketcb2poll * * convert from libcurl' CURL_POLL_* bit definitions to poll()'s */

function.name: socketcb2poll

unit.comment: /* events_socket * * Callback that gets called with information about socket activity to * monitor. */

function.name: events_socket
function.parameter_list: (struct Curl_easy *easy, /* easy handle */ curl_socket_t s, /* socket */ int what, /* see above */ void *userp, /* private callback pointer */ void *socketp)

unit.comment: /* * events_setup() * * Do the multi handle setups that only event-based transfers need. */

function.name: events_setup

unit.comment: /* wait_or_timeout() * * waits for activity on any of the given sockets, or the timeout to trigger. */

function.name: wait_or_timeout

unit.comment: /* easy_events() * * Runs a transfer in a blocking manner using the events-based API */

function.name: easy_events

unit.comment: /* CURLDEBUG */
/* when not built with debug, this function doesn't exist */

function.name: easy_transfer

unit.comment: /* * easy_perform() is the external interface that performs a blocking * transfer as previously setup. * * CONCEPT: This function creates a multi handle, adds the easy handle to it, * runs curl_multi_perform() until the transfer is done, then detaches the * easy handle, destroys the multi handle and returns the easy handle's return * code. * * REALITY: it can't just create and destroy the multi handle that easily. It * needs to keep it around since if this easy handle is used again by this * function, the same multi handle must be reused so that the same pools and * caches can be used. * * DEBUG: if 'events' is set TRUE, this function will use a replacement engine * instead of curl_multi_perform() and use curl_multi_socket_action(). */

function.name: easy_perform

unit.comment: /* * curl_easy_perform() is the external interface that performs a blocking * transfer as previously setup. */

function.name: curl_easy_perform

unit.comment: /* * curl_easy_perform_ev() is the external interface that performs a blocking * transfer using the event-based API internally. */

function.name: curl_easy_perform_ev

unit.comment: /* * curl_easy_cleanup() is the external interface to cleaning/freeing the given * easy handle. */

function.name: curl_easy_cleanup

unit.comment: /* * curl_easy_getinfo() is an external interface that allows an app to retrieve * information from a performed transfer and similar. */

function.name: curl_easy_getinfo
dupset

unit.comment: /* * curl_easy_duphandle() is an external interface to allow duplication of a * given input easy handle. The returned handle will be a new working handle * with all options set exactly as the input source handle. */

function.name: curl_easy_duphandle

unit.comment: /* * curl_easy_reset() is an external interface that allows an app to re- * initialize a session handle to the default values. */

function.name: curl_easy_reset

unit.comment: /* * curl_easy_pause() allows an application to pause or unpause a specific * transfer and direction. This function sets the full new state for the * current connection this easy handle operates on. * * NOTE: if you have the receiving paused and you call this function to remove * the pausing, you may get your write callback called at this point. * * Action is a bitmask consisting of CURLPAUSE_* bits in curl/curl.h * * NOTE: This is one of few API functions that are allowed to be called from * within a callback. */

function.name: curl_easy_pause
easy_connection

unit.comment: /* * Receives data from the connected socket. Use after successful * curl_easy_perform() with CURLOPT_CONNECT_ONLY option. * Returns CURLE_OK on success, error code on error. */

function.name: curl_easy_recv
Curl_connect_only_attach

unit.comment: /* USE_WEBSOCKETS */
/* * Sends data over the connected socket. * * This is the private internal version of curl_easy_send() */

function.name: Curl_senddata
function.parameter_list: (struct Curl_easy *data, const void *buffer, size_t buflen, size_t *n)

unit.comment: /* * Sends data over the connected socket. Use after successful * curl_easy_perform() with CURLOPT_CONNECT_ONLY option. */

function.name: curl_easy_send
function.parameter_list: (struct Curl_easy *data, const void *buffer, size_t buflen, size_t *n)

unit.comment: /* * Wrapper to call functions in Curl_conncache_foreach() * * Returns always 0. */

function.name: conn_upkeep
upkeep
curl_easy_upkeep
